[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a private cluster [OPTIONAL] Creates a public management cluster in a custom vnet
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a highly available cluster [REQUIRED] With 3 control-plane nodes and 2 Linux and 2 Windows worker nodes
[It] Workload cluster creation when Creating a highly available cluster with Azure CNI v1 [REQUIRED] can create 3 control-plane nodes and 2 Linux worker nodes [Azure CNI v1]

[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a cluster with spot vms [OPTIONAL] With spot vm machine deployments
[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a ipv6 control-plane cluster [REQUIRED] With ipv6 worker node

[It] Workload cluster creation Creating a cluster with VMSS flex machinepools [OPTIONAL] with 1 control plane node and 1 machinepool

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Running the quick-start spec Should create a workload cluster
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Running the MachineDeployment rollout spec Should successfully upgrade Machines upon changes in relevant MachineDeployment fields
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Running the self-hosted spec Should pivot the bootstrap cluster to a self-hosted cluster
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Should successfully remediate unhealthy worker machines with MachineHealthCheck Should replace unhealthy machines
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Should successfully exercise machine pools Should successfully create a cluster with machine pool machines

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Should successfully scale out and scale in a MachineDeployment Should successfully scale a MachineDeployment up and down upon changes to the MachineDeployment replica count

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests API Version Upgrade upgrade from an old version of v1beta1 to current, and scale workload clusters created in the old version Should create a management cluster and then upgrade all the providers

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests API Version Upgrade upgrade from the latest version of v1beta1 to current, and scale workload clusters created in the old version Should create a management cluster and then upgrade all the providers

[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests API Version Upgrade upgrade from the latest version of v1beta1 to current, and scale AKS workload clusters created in the old version Should create a management cluster and then upgrade all the providers
[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests Running the workload cluster upgrade spec [K8s-Upgrade] Should create and upgrade a workload cluster and eventually run kubetest

test_cases.failure_message:
Timed out after 3600.000s.
Timed out waiting for all control-plane machines in Cluster k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-a75582/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-e8ed3q to be upgraded to kubernetes version v1.35.5
The function passed to Eventually returned the following error:
    <*errors.fundamental | 0xc00072d6e0>: 
    old Machines remain
    {
        msg: "old Machines remain",
        stack: [0x44e4bf8, 0x219064a, 0x218f739, 0x3ffdb7f, 0x3ffebd7, 0x3ffbf25, 0x44e4902, 0x44d8c9e, 0x54a91c8, 0x3fd4613, 0x3feae33, 0x2104521],
    }
test_cases.failure_body:
[FAILED] Timed out after 3600.000s.
Timed out waiting for all control-plane machines in Cluster k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-a75582/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-e8ed3q to be upgraded to kubernetes version v1.35.5
The function passed to Eventually returned the following error:
    <*errors.fundamental | 0xc00072d6e0>: 
    old Machines remain
    {
        msg: "old Machines remain",
        stack: [0x44e4bf8, 0x219064a, 0x218f739, 0x3ffdb7f, 0x3ffebd7, 0x3ffbf25, 0x44e4902, 0x44d8c9e, 0x54a91c8, 0x3fd4613, 0x3feae33, 0x2104521],
    }
In [It] at: /home/prow/go/pkg/mod/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test@v1.13.2/framework/machine_helpers.go:181 @ 06/01/26 17:03:29.471

ai_summary.summary: Control plane upgrade timed out because old machines remained due to Node registration failure

ai_analysis.root_cause: Build log shows repeated "Waiting for a Node with spec.providerID... to exist" for control plane AzureMachine. AzureMachine reports VMRunning=True but Node was never registered. Likely cloud-init failure or kubelet startup issue. Evidence in build-log.txt: "NodeHealthy: Waiting for a Node with spec.providerID azure://... to exist"
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Check cloud-init logs for the control plane machine to identify bootstrapping errors. Verify AzureMachine's image version compatibility with target Kubernetes version. Ensure proper network connectivity for kubelet registration.

test_cases.name: [SynchronizedAfterSuite]
